bullets.text: Tensions escalated between the US and Iran following fresh US strikes and Iran's targeting of an American base, despite both sides reportedly seeking to avoid all-out conflict.

The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has called for an immediate ceasefire in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) to address the Ebola outbreak, warning that the spread of the disease is outpacing response efforts.
A dormitory fire at a girls' school in Kenya's Rift Valley killed at least 16 students, with parents awaiting updates on the tragedy.
The Queensland government plans to proceed with the demolition of Victoria Park in Brisbane despite challenges under the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Heritage Protection Act.

Endeavour Group, owner of major Australian liquor retailers, is selling vineyards and wineries in South Australia, Victoria, and Tasmania, and closing a large South Australian bottling facility due to industry pressures.
One Nation MPs Pauline Hanson and Barnaby Joyce claimed parliamentary travel expenses for attending private fundraising events on a luxury cruise ship hosted by mining billionaire Gina Rinehart.
Chevron's CEO forecasts a summer jump in oil prices due to dwindling supplies, with the Strait of Hormuz blockade impacting global markets and reducing available reserves.
Anthropic has finalized a $65 billion funding deal, valuing the Claude AI maker at $965 billion and surpassing OpenAI's valuation, indicating significant investment in the AI sector.
The EU is planning to introduce crisis powers that could force chipmakers to override existing contracts to seize control of chip supplies, reflecting concerns over semiconductor security.
